We have commanded man to be good to his parents: his mother struggled to carry him and struggled to give birth to him.
You [Prophet] truly have a strong character.
God created the heavens and the earth for a true purpose, so that every soul may be repaid for what it has earned.
Tell the believers to forgive those who do not look forward to the Days of God.
On that Day, even friends will be enemies to one another, except for those who were mindful of God.
Though if anyone is patient and forgives, that is a matter of high resolve.
The reward for an injury is an equal injury, but if anyone forgives and makes peace, his reward is due from God.
Those who respond to their Lord and keep up the prayer; who conduct their affairs by mutual consultation; who give to others out of what We have given them.
Whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your own hands have earned, and yet He pardons much.
He is the one who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons bad deeds. He knows everything you do.
Goodness and evil are not equal: repel evil with something better and your enemy will become as close as an old and valued friend.
Who speaks better than one who calls people to God, does what is right, and says, 'I am one of those who devote themselves to God'?
Your Lord says, 'Call on Me and I will answer you.'
Say, 'My servants who have harmed yourselves by your own excess, do not despair of God's mercy. God forgives all sins: He is truly the Most Forgiving, the Most Merciful.'
Do not lose heart or despair—if you are true believers you will have the upper hand.
No soul shall bear the burden of another.
If anyone desires power, all power belongs to God. Good words rise up to Him.
You who believe, be mindful of God, speak in a direct and appropriate manner.
For men and women who are devoted to God... God has prepared forgiveness and a rich reward.
Go at a moderate pace and lower your voice.
Do not turn your nose up at people, nor walk proudly on the earth: God does not love the arrogant or the boastful.
Keep up the prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be steadfast in whatever befalls you.
Another of His signs is that He created spouses from among yourselves for you to live with in tranquillity: He ordained love and kindness between you.
We shall guide those who strive in Our cause to Our paths: God is with the good-doers.
Keep up the prayer: prayer restrains people from shameful and evil deeds.
Do people think they will be left alone to say, 'We believe,' without being put to the test?
Seek the life to come by means of what God has granted you, but do not neglect your share of this world. Be good to others as God has been good to you.
My Lord, inspire me to be thankful for the blessings You have granted me and my parents.
Those who, when they spend, are neither wasteful nor tight-fisted, but keep to a sensible medium.
The servants of the Lord of Mercy are those who walk humbly on the earth, and who, when the foolish address them, reply, 'Peace'.
God is the Light of the heavens and the earth.
Tell believing men to lower their gaze and remain chaste: that is purer for them.
Did you think We created you in vain, and that you would not be returned to Us?
Repel evil with what is better. We know best what they say.
You who believe, bow down, prostrate yourselves, worship your Lord, and do good, so that you may prosper.
It was only as a mercy that We sent you [Prophet] to all people.
Every soul is bound to taste death. We test you through both evil and good as a trial of your faith.
We made every living thing from water. Will they not believe?
Say, 'Lord, increase my knowledge.'
The Lord of Mercy will give love to those who believe and do good deeds.
Say, 'If the whole ocean were ink for writing my Lord's words, it would run dry before those words were finished.'
Wealth and children are but adornment of the worldly life. But the enduring good deeds are better.
Never say of anything, 'I will do that tomorrow,' without adding, 'God willing.'
We have made whatever is on earth an adornment for it, to test which of them does best.
Praise be to God, who has sent down the Scripture to His servant and made it free from any distortion.
Say, 'Call on God, or on the Lord of Mercy—whatever name you use, the Most Excellent Names belong to Him.'
We send down the Qur'an as a healing and a mercy for the believers.
We have honoured the children of Adam and carried them by land and sea... and favoured them greatly over many of those We created.
Tell My servants to say what is best. Satan is always sowing discord among them.
Do not walk proudly on the earth: you cannot crack the earth apart, nor can you ever match the mountains in height.
Do not follow that of which you have no knowledge: the hearing, the sight, and the heart—all of these will be questioned.
Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with accurate scales.
Keep your pledges, for you will be questioned about your pledges.
Do not go near adultery: it is an outrage and an evil path.
Do not kill your children for fear of poverty—We provide for them and for you—killing them is a great sin.
Your Lord has commanded that you should worship none but Him, and that you be kind to your parents.
Invite [all] to the Way of your Lord with wisdom and kind advice. Argue with them in the most courteous way.
God commands justice, doing good, and generosity to relatives. He forbids what is shameful, blameworthy, and oppressive.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them without a true purpose... So overlook [their faults] with gracious forgiveness.
Do not think that God is unaware of what the wrongdoers do.
If you are grateful, I will give you more.
Truly, in the remembrance of God do hearts find rest.
Those who are steadfast... and who repel evil with good: for such as these there is the ultimate abode.
God does not change the condition of a people unless they change what is in themselves.
Judgement belongs only to God; I have put my trust in Him, and let all who trust put their trust in Him.
Patience is a virtue: God is my only help in the face of what you say.
Be steadfast: God does not let the rewards of those who do good go to waste.
Good deeds wipe out evil deeds: this is a reminder for those who pay heed.
God does not wrong people at all; it is they who wrong themselves.
It is He who made the sun a shining light and the moon a light [reflected].
You who believe, be true to your duty to God and be among the truthful.
If they incline towards peace, you [Prophet] must also incline towards it, and put your trust in God.
Respond to God and the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life.
The true believers are those whose hearts tremble with awe when God is mentioned.
When the Qur'an is recited, listen to it and be silent, so that you may be shown mercy.
Be tolerant and command what is right: pay no attention to foolish people.
The Most Excellent Names belong to God: use them to call on Him.
The earth belongs to God: He gives it as an inheritance to whichever of His servants He will. The future belongs to the righteous.
Do not cause corruption in the land after it has been set in order: call on Him with fear and hope.
Call on your Lord humbly and privately—He does not like those who exceed His limits.
Eat and drink, but do not be wasteful: God does not love the wasteful.
No soul shall bear the burden of another. In the end you will all return to your Lord.
Give full measure and weight, with justice... When you speak, be just, even if it concerns a relative.
Say, 'Come, I will tell you what your Lord has really forbidden you: Do not associate anything with Him; be good to your parents...'
It is He who produces gardens... Eat of their fruit when they bear fruit... but do not be wasteful: God does not love those who are wasteful.
If you obeyed most of those on earth, they would lead you away from the path of God. They follow nothing but conjecture.
Say, 'I do not tell you that I have God's treasures, nor that I know the unseen... I only follow what is revealed to me.'
What is the life of this world but a game and a distraction? The home in the Hereafter is best for those who are mindful of God.
If God touches you with affliction, no one can remove it but Him; and if He touches you with good, He has power over all things.
You who believe, you are responsible for your own souls; if you are guided, those who have gone astray will not harm you.
We have assigned a law and a path to each of you. If God had so willed, He would have made you one community.
If anyone saves a life, it is as if he saves the lives of all mankind.
Do not let hatred of others lead you away from justice, but be just, for that is closer to awareness of God.
Help one another in goodness and provision, and do not help one another in sin and aggression.
Whether you do good openly or in private, or pardon an evil, God is all pardoning and all powerful.
You who believe, be steadfast in justice and bear witness for the sake of God, even if it is against yourselves, your parents, or your close relatives.
There is no good in much of their secret talk, except in the case of those who enjoin charity and kindness, or reconciliation between people.
Worship God and do not associate anything with Him. Be good to your parents, to relatives, orphans, the needy...
Do not covet the bounties which God has bestowed more abundantly on some of you than on others.
Those who remember God standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth.
You are sure to be tested through your possessions and persons... but if you are steadfast and mindful of God, that is the best course.
It was by God's mercy that you [Prophet] were gentle with them: for if you had been harsh and hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from around you.
Those who give, both in prosperity and adversity, who restrain their anger and pardon people—God loves those who do good.
Hold fast to God's rope all together; do not be divided. Remember the favour He bestowed on you when you were enemies and He brought your hearts together.
They do not beg importunately from people. Whatever good you give, God knows it well.
He gives wisdom to whoever He will. Whoever is given wisdom has truly been given much good.
There is no compulsion in religion: true guidance has become distinct from error.
Do you suppose that you will enter the Garden without first having suffered like those before you?
The life of this world is made to seem glamorous to the disbelievers... but those who are mindful of God will be above them on the Day of Resurrection.
Whatever good you do, God is aware of it. Take provision, but the best provision is mindfulness of God.
You who believe, seek help through steadfastness and prayer, for God is with the steadfast.
Each community has its own direction to which it turns: race to do good deeds and wherever you are, God will bring you all together.
The East and the West belong to God: wherever you turn, there is His Face. God is all pervading, all knowing.
Whoever does a good deed will be repaid tenfold, but whoever does an evil deed will be repaid only with its equivalent.
In the name of God, the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy!
Truly, in the remembrance of God do hearts find rest.
God does not change the condition of a people (for the worse) unless they change what is in themselves.
Be tolerant and command what is right: pay no attention to foolish people.

Say, 'Come, I will tell you what your Lord has really forbidden you: Do not associate anything with Him; be good to your parents; do not kill your children for fear of poverty—We shall provide for you and for them; do not go near lewdness, whether open or secret; and do not kill the soul which God has made sacred, except by right.'

If anyone kills a person—unless in retribution for murder or spreading corruption in the land—it is as if he kills all mankind, while if anyone saves a life, it is as if he saves the lives of all mankind.

You who believe, be steadfast in justice and bear witness for the sake of God, even if it is against yourselves, your parents, or your close relatives. Whether the person is rich or poor, God can best take care of both. Do not let your desires cause you to deviate (from justice). If you distort your testimony or refuse to give it, God is well aware of what you do.
You will never attain true piety until you give from what you cherish: whatever you give, God knows about it very well.

True righteousness is to believe in God and the Last Day, the angels, the Scripture, and the prophets; to give out of one's cherished wealth to relatives and orphans, the needy, the wayfarer, beggars, and for the freeing of slaves; those who keep up the prayer and pay the prescribed alms; who keep pledges whenever they make them; who are steadfast in misfortune, adversity, and times of danger. These are the ones who are true, and it is they who are aware of God.
In the name of God, the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy!
By the declining day, man is (deep) in loss, except for those who believe, do good deeds, urge one another to the truth, and urge one another to steadfastness.
Whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it. And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.
Everything on earth is bound to perish, but your Lord's face will remain full of majesty and glory.
We created man—We know what his soul whispers to him: We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
Goodness and evil are not equal: repel evil with something better and your enemy will become as close as an old and valued friend.
The servants of the Lord of Mercy are those who walk humbly on the earth, and who, when the foolish address them, reply, 'Peace'.
Wealth and children are but adornment of the worldly life. But the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for one's hope.
Do not walk proudly on the earth: you cannot crack the earth apart, nor can you ever match the mountains in height.

Your Lord has commanded that you should worship none but Him, and that you be kind to your parents. If either or both of them reach old age with you, say no word that shows impatience with them, and do not be harsh with them, but speak to them respectfully.

Kind speech and forgiveness are better than charity followed by injury. And Allah is Free of need and Forbearing.

There shall be no compulsion in acceptance of the religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong. So whoever disbelieves in Taghut and believes in Allah has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no break in it. And Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.
Is the reward for good anything but good?

Know that the life of this world is but amusement and diversion and adornment and boasting to one another and competition in increase of wealth and children. And what is the worldly life except the enjoyment of delusion.
Do you order righteousness of the people and forget yourselves while you recite the Scripture? Then will you not reason?
We have sent you with the truth, as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner. You will not be asked about the inhabitants of the Blazing Fire.

Remember when Abraham was tested by his Lord with certain commandments, which he fulfilled. He said, "I will make you a leader of people." Abraham asked, "And my descendants?" God said, "My pledge does not hold for those who do evil."
Abraham and Ishmael built the foundations of the House: "Our Lord, accept this from us. You are the All Hearing, the All Knowing."
"Our Lord, make us both devoted to You; make our descendants into a community devoted to You. Show us our holy rites and accept our repentance, for You are the Relenting, the Merciful."
"Our Lord, make a messenger of their own rise up from among them, to recite Your revelations to them, teach them the Scripture and wisdom, and purify them: You are the Mighty, the Wise."
Who but a fool would forsake the religion of Abraham? We chose him in this world, and in the next he will be among the righteous.
When his Lord said to him, "Devote yourself to Me," he replied, "I devote myself to the Lord of the Worlds."
Abraham commanded his sons to do this, as did Jacob: "My sons, God has chosen this religion for you, so do not die except in devotion to Him."
That community has passed away: what they earned belongs to them, and what you earn belongs to you. You will not be answerable for their deeds.

Say (believers), "We believe in God and in what was sent down to us and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, and what was given to Moses, Jesus, and all the prophets by their Lord."
The foolish people will say, "What has turned them away from the prayer direction they used to face?" Say, "The East and the West belong to God. He guides whoever He will to a straight path."
So remember Me; I will remember you. Be thankful to Me, and never ungrateful.
You who believe, seek help through steadfastness and prayer, for God is with the steadfast.
Do not say that those who are killed in God’s cause are dead; they are alive, though you do not realize it.
We shall certainly test you with fear and hunger, and loss of property, lives, and crops. But (Prophet), give good news to those who are steadfast
those who say, when afflicted with a calamity, "We belong to God and to Him we shall return." These are the people who will have blessings and mercy from their Lord.
Your God is the one God: there is no god but Him, the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy.
There is guidance in the creation of the heavens and the earth, in the alternation of night and day, in the ships that sail the sea with goods for people.
When they are told, "Follow what God has sent down," they say, "We will follow what we found our fathers doing," even though their fathers had no understanding and no guidance.
Righteousness does not consist in turning your faces towards the East or the West.

True righteousness is to believe in God and the Last Day, the angels, the Scripture, and the prophets; to give out of one’s cherished wealth to relatives and orphans, the needy, the wayfarer, beggars, and for the freeing of slaves

those who keep up the prayer and pay the prescribed alms; who keep pledges whenever they make them; who are steadfast in misfortune, adversity, and times of danger. These are the ones who are true, and it is they who are aware of God.
You who believe, fasting is prescribed for you, as it was prescribed for those before you, so that you may be mindful of God.
(Prophet), if My servants ask you about Me, I am near. I respond to those who call Me, so let them respond to Me, and believe in Me, so that they may be guided.
Do not consume each other’s wealth unjustly, nor offer it to judges in order to consume a portion of other people’s wealth sinfully, even though you know (it is wrong).
Fight in God’s cause against those who fight you, but do not overstep the limits: God does not love those who overstep the limits.
Give freely in God’s cause and do not with your own hands throw yourselves into destruction. Do good, for God loves those who do good.
There are some people who say, "Our Lord, give us abundance in this world," but they will have no share in the world to come.

Others say, "Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the next, and keep us safe from the pain of the Fire." These people will have a share of what they have earned: God is swift in settling accounts.
There is a man whose views on the life of this world may please you; he even calls on God to witness what is in his heart, yet he is the bitterest of opponents.
When he leaves you, he quickly spreads corruption in the land and destroys crops and livestock. God does not love corruption.

The life of this world is made to seem glamorous to the disbelievers, and they laugh at those who believe. But those who are mindful of God will be above them on the Day of Resurrection: God provides immeasurably for whoever He will.
Mankind was a single community, then God sent prophets to bring good news and warnings, and with them He sent the Scripture with the truth, to judge between people in their disagreements.
They ask you what they should give: say, "Whatever good you give should be for parents, close relatives, orphans, the needy, and travellers. God is well aware of whatever good you do."
Fighting is ordained for you, though you dislike it. You may dislike something although it is good for you, or love something although it is bad for you: God knows and you do not.
Those who have believed, migrated, and striven for God’s cause, it is they who can look forward to God’s mercy: God is most forgiving and most merciful.
They ask you (Prophet) about intoxicants and gambling: say, "There is great sin in both, and some benefit for people: the sin is greater than the benefit."
They ask you what they should give: say, "Give what you can spare." In this way, God makes His messages clear to you, so that you may reflect on this world and the next.
They ask you about orphans: say, "It is good to set things right for them. If you combine your affairs with theirs, remember they are your brothers."
Do not marry idolatresses until they believe: a believing slave woman is certainly better than an idolatress, even though she may please you.
Do not use God’s name in your oaths as an excuse for not being righteous, not being mindful of God, and not putting things right between people. God is all hearing, all knowing.
God will not call you to account for your unintended oaths, but He will call you to account for what is in your hearts. God is most forgiving and forbearing.

If you divorce them before you have touched them but after you have settled the marriage gift, give them half of what you settled, unless they or the man in whose hand is the marriage tie should waive it.
To waive it is closer to being mindful of God. Do not forget to be generous to one another: God sees what you do.
Guard your prayers, and the middle prayer, and stand before God in total devotion.
When they are told, "Do not cause corruption in the land," they say, "We are only putting things right," but it is they who are causing corruption, though they do not realize it.

There are some who say, "We believe in God and the Last Day," when in fact they believe in nothing. They seek to deceive God and the believers, but they only deceive themselves, though they do not realize it.
Praise belongs to God, Lord of the Worlds, the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy, Master of the Day of Judgement. It is You we worship; it is You we ask for help.
As for those who disbelieve, it makes no difference whether you warn them or not: they will not believe. God has sealed their hearts and their ears, and their eyes are covered.
those who believe in the revelation sent down to you (Muhammad), and in what was sent before you, and believe firmly in the life to come.
Guide us to the straight path: the path of those You have blessed, those who incur no anger and who have not gone astray.
This is the Scripture in which there is no doubt, containing guidance for those who are mindful of God, who believe in the unseen, keep up the prayer, and give out of what We have provided for them
A kind word and forgiveness are better than a charitable deed followed by hurtful words: God is self-sufficient, forbearing.

Those who spend their wealth in God’s cause are like grains of corn that produce seven ears, each containing a hundred grains. God gives multiple increase to whoever He will: God is all embracing, all knowing.
Abraham said, "My Lord, show me how You give life to the dead." He said, "Do you not believe?" Abraham said, "Yes, but I wish to set my heart at rest."

God is the ally of those who believe: He brings them out of the depths of darkness and into the light. As for the disbelievers, their allies are false gods who take them from the light into the depths of darkness.
There is no compulsion in religion: true guidance has become distinct from error, so whoever rejects false gods and believes in God has grasped the firmest hand-hold, one that will never break.

He knows what is before them and what is behind them, but they do not comprehend any of His knowledge except what He wills. His throne extends over the heavens and the earth; it does not weary Him to preserve them both.
God: there is no god but Him, the Ever Living, the Ever Watchful. Neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him. All that is in the heavens and in the earth belongs to Him.
You who believe, give from what We have provided for you, before the Day comes when there is no bargaining, no friendship, and no intercession. It is the disbelievers who are wrongdoers.
Those were the messengers. We made some of them excel others: to some God spoke, and some He raised in rank. We gave Jesus, son of Mary, clear signs and supported him with the Holy Spirit.
"How many a small group has defeated a large group by God’s leave! God is with the steadfast."
He said, "God has chosen him over you, and has given him great knowledge and physical strength. God gives His kingship to whoever He will: God is all embracing, all knowing."

Their prophet said to them, "God has appointed Saul to be your king." They said, "How can he have kingship over us when we are more deserving of it than him, and he has not even been given much wealth?"
Who will give God a good loan, which He will increase for him many times over? It is God who withholds and God who gives abundantly, and it is to Him that you will return.
Do they not know that God knows what they conceal and what they reveal?

God is not unaware of what you do. Do you (believers) really hope that they will believe you, when some of them used to hear the words of God and then, after understanding them, knowingly distorted them?

Then your hearts became hard like rocks, or even harder: for there are some rocks from which streams spring out, and some which split open and water pours out, and some which fall down for fear of God.
Moses said to his people, "God commands you to sacrifice a cow." They said, "Are you making fun of us?" He answered, "God forbid that I should be so ignorant."

Indeed, those who believe, and those who are Jews and Christians, and Sabians—any who believe in God and the Last Day and do good—shall have their reward with their Lord; no fear shall come upon them, nor shall they grieve.
And We shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails, saying, "Eat of the good things We have provided for you." They did not wrong Us; it was themselves they were wronging.
Remember when you said, "Moses, we will not believe you until we see God actually with our own eyes," and the thunderbolt struck you while you were looking on.
Remember when We gave Moses the Scripture and the Criterion, so that you might be guided.
Guard yourselves against a Day when no soul will stand in place of another, no intercession will be accepted for it, nor any ransom taken, nor will they be helped.
Seek help with steadfastness and prayer—though this is hard indeed for anyone but the humble, who know that they will meet their Lord and that it is to Him they will return.
How can you tell people to do what is right and forget to do it yourselves, even though you recite the Scripture? Have you no sense?
Do not shroud the truth with falsehood, nor knowingly conceal the truth. Keep up the prayer, pay the prescribed alms, and bow down with those who bow.
Children of Israel, remember the favour I showed you, and keep your pledge to Me and I will keep Mine to you. Be afraid of Me (alone).
We said, "Go down from it, all of you! If guidance comes to you from Me, then those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve."
Then Adam received some words from his Lord and He accepted his repentance: He is the Relenting, the Merciful.
But Satan made them slip, and removed them from the state they were in. We said, "Go down, all of you, as enemies to each other! On earth you will have a place to stay and livelihood for a time."
We said, "Adam, live with your wife in this garden. Both of you eat freely there as you will, but do not go near this tree, or you will both become wrongdoers."
They said, "May You be glorified! We have no knowledge other than what You have taught us. You alone are the All Knowing, the All Wise."
He taught Adam all the names (of things), then He showed them to the angels and said, "Tell me the names of these if you are right."
Remember when We took a pledge from the Children of Israel: "Worship none but God; be good to your parents and kinsfolk, to orphans and the poor; speak kindly to people."

Truly, those who do evil and are surrounded by their sins will be the inhabitants of the Fire, there to remain, while those who believe and do good deeds will be the inhabitants of the Garden, there to remain.

So woe to those who write the Scripture with their own hands and then say, "This is from God," in order to make a small gain. Woe to them for what their hands have written! Woe to them for what they have earned!
Among them are uneducated people who know nothing of the Scripture except lies: they do nothing but guess.

When your Lord said to the angels, "I am putting a successor on earth," they said, "Will You place someone there who will cause corruption on it and shed blood, while we celebrate Your praise and proclaim Your holiness?"
He is the one who created everything on earth for you, then turned to the sky and made the seven heavens; He has full knowledge of all things.
How can you disbelieve in God when you were dead and He gave you life? Then He will let you die, then He will bring you to life again and then you will be returned to Him.
Give those who believe and do good the good news that they will have Gardens graced with flowing streams.
He who made the earth a habitable place for you and the sky a structure; who sent down water from the sky and with it brought forth fruits for your sustenance.
People, worship your Lord, who created you and those before you, so that you may be mindful of Him
When they meet the believers, they say, "We believe," but when they are alone with their evil ones, they say, "We are really with you; we were only mocking."
Whatever good you send forth for your own souls, you will find it with God: God sees all that you do.
They say, "No one will enter Paradise unless he is a Jew or a Christian." This is their own wishful thinking. (Prophet), say, "Produce your evidence if you are telling the truth."
In fact, any who directs himself wholly to God and does good will have his reward with his Lord: no fear shall come upon them, nor shall they grieve.
The East and the West belong to God: wherever you turn, there is His Face. God is all pervading, all knowing.
The Originator of the heavens and the earth! When He decrees a matter, He says only, "Be," and it is.
He is the one who shapes you in the womb as He pleases; there is no God but Him, the Mighty, the Wise.
Say, 'My prayer and my sacrifice, my life and my death, are all for God, Lord of the Worlds; He has no partner. This is what I am commanded, and I am the first to devote myself to Him.'
Whoever does a good deed will be repaid tenfold, but whoever does an evil deed will be repaid only with its equivalent: they will not be wronged.
When you speak, be just, even if it concerns a relative; and keep your pledge to God. This is what He commands you, so that you may take heed.
Do not go near the orphan's property, except with the best [intentions], until they reach maturity. Give full measure and weight, with justice.
do not go near lewdness, whether open or secret; and do not kill the soul which God has made sacred, except by right. This is what He commands you, so that you may understand.

Say, 'Come, I will tell you what your Lord has really forbidden you: Do not associate anything with Him; be good to your parents; do not kill your children for fear of poverty—We shall provide for you and for them'
God does not love those who are wasteful.
Now clear evidence has come to you from your Lord. Whoever sees will do so for his own soul's good, and whoever is blind will be so to his own soul's detriment.
It is He who produced you from a single soul, then gave you a place to stay [in life] and a resting place [after death]. We have made Our revelations clear to those who understand.
This is God's guidance: He guides whoever He will of His servants with it.
Leave to themselves those who take their religion for a game and a distraction, and who are deluded by the life of this world.
Every prophecy has its appointed time: you will come to know.
Say, 'It is God who saves you from these and from all other distresses, yet you still associate others with Him.'
He is the one who takes your souls by night and knows what you do by day, then He raises you up again during the day until the appointed term is completed.
What is the life of this world but a game and a distraction? The home in the Hereafter is best for those who are mindful of God. Do you not understand?
If God touches you with affliction, no one can remove it but Him; and if He touches you with good, He has power over all things.
Say, 'Should I take any protector other than God, the Creator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds all and is not fed by any?'
Say, 'To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and on earth?' Say, 'To God. He has taken it upon Himself to be merciful.'
If We had sent down to you a scripture on parchment, and they had touched it with their own hands, the disbelievers would still have said, 'This is nothing but plain sorcery.'
He is God in the heavens and on earth! He knows what you keep secret and what you reveal; He knows what you earn.
It is He who created you from clay and then decreed a term [of life]—a term known only to Him—yet still you doubt.
Everything in the heavens and the earth belongs to God; and He has power over all things.
God said, 'This is a Day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness. They will have Gardens graced with flowing streams, there to remain for ever.'
Remember when the disciples said, 'Jesus, son of Mary, can your Lord send down a feast to us from heaven?' He said, 'Be mindful of God, if you are true believers.'

[Prophet], remember when God said, 'Jesus, son of Mary, remember My favour to you and to your mother: how I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit, so that you spoke to people in your infancy and in your adulthood'
You who believe, you are responsible for your own souls; if you are guided, those who have gone astray will not harm you.
You who believe, do not ask about things which, if made plain to you, may cause you trouble.
Say, 'Bad cannot be equal to good, even though the abundance of the bad may please you.' So be mindful of God, people of understanding, so that you may prosper.
The Messenger's duty is only to deliver the message: God knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
Obey God and obey the Messenger, and be on your guard.
By means of intoxicants and gambling, Satan seeks only to incite enmity and hatred among you, and to stop you from remembering God and prayer. Will you not give them up?
You who believe, intoxicants, gambling, idolatrous practices, and [divining with] arrows are repugnant acts—Satan's doing—so avoid them so that you may prosper.
God will not call you to account for your unintended oaths, but He will call you to account for the oaths which you make in earnest.
You who believe, do not forbid the good things God has made lawful to you—do not exceed the limits: God does not love those who exceed the limits.
Those who ally themselves with God, His Messenger, and the believers [will belong to] God's party, and they are the ones who will triumph.
Your true allies are God, His Messenger, and the believers—those who keep up the prayer, pay the prescribed alms, and bow down in worship.
Do they want the judgment of the Time of Ignorance? Who could be a better judge than God for people who are sure of their faith?
They will be returned to God, and He will inform them about their differences.
We have assigned a law and a path to each of you. If God had so willed, He would have made you one community, but He wanted to test you through that which He has given you, so race to do good.
[Prophet], We have, then, sent you the Scripture with the truth, confirming the previous Scriptures and as a criterion for them. So judge between them according to what God has sent down.
You who believe, be mindful of God, seek ways to come closer to Him and strive for His cause, so that you may prosper.

On that account We ordained for the Children of Israel that if anyone kills a person—unless in retribution for murder or spreading corruption in the land—it is as if he kills all mankind, while if anyone saves a life, it is as if he saves the lives of all mankind.
You who believe, be steadfast in your devotion to God and bear witness impartially: do not let hatred of others lead you away from justice, but be just, for that is closer to awareness of God.
When you are offered a greeting, respond with a better one, or at least return it: God keeps count of everything.
Whoever speaks for a good cause will share in its reward, and whoever speaks for a bad cause will share in its burden: God keeps watch over everything.
Why do they not reflect on the Qur'an? If it had been from anyone other than God, they would have found many inconsistencies in it.
Whoever obeys the Messenger obeys God. If some turn away, We have not sent you to be their keeper.
Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even if you are in lofty towers.
Whoever obeys God and the Messenger will be in the company of those God has blessed: the prophets, the truthful, the witnesses, and the righteous. What excellent companions these are!
If they had done as they were told, it would have been better for them and more effective in strengthening their faith.

God commands you [people] to return things entrusted to you to their rightful owners, and, if you judge between people, to do so with justice: God's instructions to you are excellent, for He hears and sees everything.

Men shall have a share in what their parents and closest relatives leave, and women shall have a share in what their parents and closest relatives leave, whether the legacy be small or large: this is a determinant share.
When you hand over their property to them, call witnesses in their presence; God is enough to take account of everything.
Do not entrust your property which God has made a means of support for you to those who are immature, but feed and clothe them from it and speak to them with kind words.
Give women their bridal gift upon marriage: though if they are happy to give up some of it for you, you may enjoy it with a clear conscience.
Give orphans their property, do not replace their good things with bad, and do not consume their property by mixing it with your own, for this is a great sin.
People, be mindful of your Lord, who created you from a single soul, and from it created its mate, and from the pair of them spread countless men and women far and wide.
You who believe, be steadfast, more steadfast than others; be ready; and be mindful of God, so that you may prosper.
'Our Lord! You have not created all this without purpose—You are far above that!—so save us from the torment of the Fire.'
those who remember God standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth
In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, there are signs for people of understanding
The life of this world is nothing but an illusory pleasure.
Every soul is bound to taste death: you will be given your full rewards only on the Day of Resurrection. Whoever is kept away from the Fire and admitted to the Garden will have triumphed
Do not think of those who have been killed in God's cause as dead. They are alive, and being provided for by their Lord
If God helps you, no one can overcome you; if He forsakes you, who is there that can help you after Him? So let the believers put their trust in God.
It was by God's mercy that you [Prophet] were gentle with them: for if you had been harsh and hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from around you.
How many a prophet has fought with many devout men alongside him! They did not lose heart at what they suffered in God's cause, nor did they weaken or give in: God loves the steadfast.
those who give, both in prosperity and adversity, who restrain their anger and pardon people—God loves those who do good
Hurry towards your Lord's forgiveness and a Garden as wide as the heavens and earth prepared for the righteous
Whatever good they do will not be ignored: God is fully aware of those who are mindful of Him.
[Believers], you are the best community singled out for people: you order what is right, forbid what is wrong, and believe in God.

Hold fast to God's rope all together; do not be divided. Remember the favour He bestowed on you when you were enemies and He brought your hearts together, so that through His favour you became brothers.
Be mindful of God, as is His due, and do not die except devoted to Him.
The first House [of worship] to be established for mankind was the one at Becca [Mecca]. It is a blessed place; a source of guidance for all people
You will never attain true piety until you give from what you cherish: whatever you give, God knows about it very well.
He is the Supreme Master over His servants: He is the All Wise, the All Aware.
Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian. He was a man of pure faith, one who surrendered himself to God; he was not one of the idolaters.
People of the Book, why do you argue about Abraham when the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until after his time? Have you no sense?

Say, 'People of the Book, let us come to a common word between us and you: that we worship none but God, that we associate nothing with Him, and that none of us shall take others as lords beside God.'
Prophet, if anyone argues with you about this after the knowledge you have received, say, 'Come, let us gather our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves'
'God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path.'
'My Lord, how can I have a son when I am so old and my wife is barren?' He said, 'It will be so: God does whatever He will.'
Say, 'If you love God [Prophet], follow me, and God will love you and forgive you your sins. God is most forgiving, most merciful.'
Proclaim: 'Whether you conceal what is in your hearts or reveal it, God knows it: He knows everything in the heavens and the earth; God has power over all things.'
God bears witness that there is no god but Him, as do the angels and those who have knowledge. He upholds justice. There is no god but Him, the Mighty, the Wise.
'Our Lord, do not let our hearts swerve now that You have guided us; give us Your mercy: You are the Ever Giving.'
'Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our Protector, so help us against the disbelievers.'
'Our Lord, do not take us to task if we forget or make a mistake. Our Lord, do not burden us as You burdened those before us. Our Lord, do not burden us with more than we have strength to bear.'
God does not burden any soul with more than it can bear: each will enjoy what good he does and suffer what evil he does.
The Messenger believes in what has been sent down to him from his Lord, as do the faithful. They all believe in God, His angels, His scriptures, and His messengers.
Whether you reveal what is in your hearts or conceal it, God will call you to account for it. He forgives whoever He will and punishes whoever He will: God has power over all things.
Be mindful of the Day when you will return to God, and every soul will be paid in full for what it has earned, and no one will be wronged.
If the debtor is in difficulty, then let there be a delay until he is in easier circumstances; but if you waive it as an act of charity, that will be better for you, if only you knew.
You who believe, be mindful of God and give up what remains due to you from usury, if you are true believers.
Those who believe, do good deeds, keep up the prayer, and pay the prescribed alms will have their reward with their Lord: no fear shall come upon them, nor shall they grieve.
Whatever good you give is for your own benefit. What you give should be only out of a desire to please God: whatever good you give will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.
You who believe, do not cancel out your charitable deeds with reminders and hurtful words, like someone who spends his wealth only to be seen by people, not believing in God and the Last Day.
Eyes cannot reach Him, but He reaches the eyes. He is the Subtle, the All Aware.
If other relatives, orphans, or needy people are present at the distribution of an inheritance, give them something from it too, and speak to them with kind words.
The servants of the Lord of Mercy are those who walk humbly on the earth, and who, when the foolish address them, reply, 'Peace'
The reward for an injury is an equal injury, but if anyone forgives and makes peace, his reward is due from God: He does not love the wrongdoers.
Whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your own hands have earned, and yet He pardons much.
He is the one who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons bad deeds. He knows everything you do.
Say, 'My Lord would not care about you at all if it were not for your prayers.'
[The servants of the Lord of Mercy are] those who, when they are reminded of their Lord’s revelations, do not fall down before them as if they were deaf and blind
[The servants of the Lord of Mercy are] those who do not bear false witness, and when they pass by some vain talk, they pass by with dignity
[The servants of the Lord of Mercy are] those who do not call upon any other god beside God, nor kill the soul which God has made sacred
It is He who has made you successors on the earth and has raised some of you above others in rank, to test you through what He has given you.
Your Lord is swift in punishment, yet He is most forgiving and most merciful.
Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord and do not follow other masters beside Him. How little you take heed!
The weighing [of deeds] that Day will be true: those whose good deeds are heavy will be the successful ones, but those whose good deeds are light will have lost their souls
We established you on the earth and provided you with a means of livelihood there. How little thanks you give!
We created you and gave you shape, then We said to the angels, 'Bow down before Adam,' and they all bowed down, except for Iblis: he was not one of those who bowed down.
God said, 'What prevented you from bowing down when I commanded you?' He said, 'I am better than him: You created me from fire and him from clay.'
[God] said, 'Get down from here! This is no place for you to be arrogant. Get out! You are one of the humiliated.'
[Iblis] said, 'Give me respite until the Day they are raised up.' [God] said, 'You have your respite.'
Children of Adam, We have sent down to you clothing to cover your nakedness, and as adornment; but the clothing of mindfulness of God is best of all.
Children of Adam, dress well whenever you at any place of worship; eat and drink, but do not be wasteful: God does not love the wasteful.
Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden lewdness, whether open or secret, and sin, and unjustified aggression'
For every community there is an appointed time: when their time comes, they can neither delay it for a moment nor can they bring it forward.
We do not burden any soul with more than it can bear.
Call on your Lord humbly and privately—He does not like those who exceed His limits.
Do not cause corruption in the land after it has been set in order: call on Him with fear and hope. God’s mercy is close to those who do good.
It is He who sends the winds as glad tidings, preceding His mercy, so that when they have carried a heavy cloud, We drive it to a dead land and send down water with it
Vegetation comes out of good land by its Lord’s leave, but from poor land only scanty things come out. In this way We explain Our revelations in various ways to those who are thankful.
If the people of those towns had believed and been mindful of God, We would have showered them with blessings from the heavens and the earth, but they rejected the truth
Do they feel secure against God’s plan? No one feels secure against God’s plan except those who are lost.
Moses said to his people, 'Turn to God for help and be steadfast. The earth belongs to God: He gives it as an inheritance to whichever of His servants He will. The future belongs to the righteous.'
When the Qur’an is recited, listen to it and be silent, so that you may be shown mercy.
Remember your Lord inwardly, in all humility and awe, without raising your voice, morning and evening—do not be one of the heedless.
The [true] believers are those whose hearts tremble with awe when God is mentioned, and whose faith is strengthened when His revelations are recited to them.
It was not you who killed them; it was God. When you threw [sand], it was not you who threw, but God, so that He might test the believers with a good test.
You who believe, obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him now that you have heard him.
Do not be like those who say, 'We have heard,' while they do not listen.
The worst of creatures in God’s sight are those who are [wilfully] deaf and dumb, who do not use their reason.
You who believe, respond to God and the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. Know that God stands between a man and his heart, and that it is to Him you will be gathered.
Know that your possessions and your children are only a test, and that God has a great reward with Him.
If you are mindful of God, He will give you a criterion [to tell right from wrong], and will wipe out your bad deeds and forgive you: God’s bounty is great indeed.
God would not punish them while you [Prophet] were among them, nor would He punish them while they were seeking forgiveness.
If they incline towards peace, you [Prophet] must also incline towards it, and put your trust in God: He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.
If they intend to deceive you, God is enough for you.
It is He who strengthened you with His help and with the believers, and brought their hearts together.
If you had spent all the wealth in the world, you could not have brought their hearts together in this way, but God brought them together: He is Mighty and Wise.
Prophet, tell the captives you have: 'If God finds any good in your hearts, He will give you something better than what has been taken from you, and He will forgive you.'
The repentance accepted by God is only for those who do evil out of ignorance and then soon repent.
It is not repentance for those who continue to do evil until death is upon one of them and he says, 'Now I repent,' nor for those who die as disbelievers.
Treat them [women] with kindness; even if you dislike them, it may be that you dislike something in which God has placed much good.
God wishes to make His laws clear to you and guide you to the righteous ways of those who went before you. He wishes to turn towards you in mercy: God is all knowing and wise.
God wishes to lighten your burden, for man was created weak.
If you avoid the great sins you have been forbidden, We shall wipe out your minor misdeeds and let you in through an entrance of honour.
Do not covet the bounties which God has bestowed more abundantly on some of you than on others. Men shall have a share of what they earn, and women shall have a share of what they earn.
Ask God for His bounty: God has full knowledge of everything.
Worship God and do not associate anything with Him. Be good to your parents, to relatives, orphans, the needy, close neighbours, distant neighbours, companions, travellers, and your slaves.
Every soul will be held responsible for what it has done: no soul shall bear the burden of another. In the end you will all return to your Lord, and He will tell you about your differences.
God does not love anyone who is arrogant and boastful
those who are miserly and tell others to be miserly, and who conceal the bounties God has given them.
What harm would it do them to believe in God and the Last Day, and to give charitably from what God has provided for them? God knows them well.
God does not wrong anyone by as much as the weight of a speck of dust: He doubles any good deed and gives a tremendous reward of His own.
God does not forgive the joining of partners with Him, but He forgives whoever He will for anything less than that. Whoever joins partners with God has strayed far, far away.
You who believe, be steadfast in justice and bear witness for the sake of God, even if it is against yourselves, your parents, or your close relatives.
Whether the person is rich or poor, God can best take care of both. Do not let your desires cause you to deviate [from justice].
If you distort your testimony or refuse to give it, God is well aware of what you do.
Those who believe, then disbelieve, then believe again, then disbelieve, and then become more and more disbelieving, will not be forgiven by God, nor will He guide them to any path.
Why should God make you suffer if you are thankful and believe in Him? God is always rewarding, all knowing.
God does not like any evil to be mentioned in public, except by someone who has been wronged: God is all hearing, all knowing.
Whether you do good openly or in private, or pardon an evil, God is all pardoning and all powerful.
Messengers who brought good news and warnings, so that mankind would have no excuse before God after the messengers. God is Mighty and Wise.
People, the Messenger has come to you with the truth from your Lord, so believe—that is better for you.
People, a convincing proof has now come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down a clear light to you.
As for those who believe in God and hold fast to Him, He will admit them to His mercy and grace and guide them to Himself by a straight path.
You who believe, be true to your duty to God and be among the truthful.
God does not change the condition of a people [for the worse] unless they change what is in themselves.
Truly, in the remembrance of God do hearts find rest.
Everything on earth is bound to perish, but your Lord’s face will remain full of majesty and glory.
By the declining day, man is [deep] in loss, except for those who believe, do good deeds, urge one another to the truth, and urge one another to steadfastness.
We created man—We know what his soul whispers to him: We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
Goodness and evil are not equal: repel evil with something better and your enemy will become as close as an old and valued friend.
Your Lord has commanded that you should worship none but Him, and that you be kind to your parents. If either or both of them reach old age with you, say no word that shows impatience with them
Do not be harsh with them, but speak to them respectfully. Lower the wing of humility to them out of mercy and say, 'Lord, have mercy on them, just as they cared for me when I was little.'
Your Lord knows best what is in your hearts. If you are righteous, He is most forgiving to those who turn to Him.
Give relatives their due, and also the needy and the traveller, but do not be wasteful: the wasteful are the brothers of the devils, and the Devil is truly ungrateful to his Lord.
Do not be tight-fisted, nor so open-handed that you end up blamed and overwhelmed with regret.
Your Lord gives abundantly to whoever He will, and sparingly to whoever He will: He is always aware of His servants and sees them all.
Do not kill your children for fear of poverty—We may provide for them and for you—killing them is a great sin.
Do not go near adultery: it is an outrage and an evil path.
Do not kill the soul which God has made sacred, except by right.
[The servants of the Lord of Mercy are] those who spend their nights prostrating and standing before their Lord
Do not go near the orphan’s property, except with the best [intentions], until he reaches maturity. Keep your pledges, for you will be questioned about your pledges.
Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with accurate scales: that is better and will have a superior outcome.
Do not follow that of which you have no knowledge: the hearing, the sight, and the heart—all of these will be questioned.
Do not walk proudly on the earth: you cannot crack the earth apart, nor can you ever match the mountains in height.
The evil of all these is hateful to your Lord. This is some of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you.
We have explained things in various ways in this Qur’an, so that they may take heed, but it only increases their aversion.
Praise be to God, who has sent down the Scripture to His servant and made it free from any distortion
We have made whatever is on earth an adornment for it, to test which of them does best.
[The servants of the Lord of Mercy are] those who, when they spend, are neither wasteful nor tight-fisted, but keep to a sensible medium
Say, 'The truth has come from your Lord. Let anyone who wishes believe it, and let anyone who wishes reject it.'
Wealth and children are the adornment of this world’s life, but lasting good deeds are better in your Lord’s sight for reward and better for hope.
We did not send any messenger before you who did not eat food and walk in the marketplaces. We have made some of you a test for others—will you be steadfast?
